name: list_log_label_values
description: List all values for a given log label in the logs matching the provided filters. This can be used to discover what values are available for filtering logs using the list_logs tool. You can query for logs across multiple resources, but all resources must be in the same region and belong to the same owner.

direction.description: The direction to query logs for. Backward will return most recent logs first. Forward will start with the oldest logs in the time range.

endTime.description: End time for log query (RFC3339 format). Defaults to the current time. The end time must be within the last 30 days.

host.description: Filter request logs by their host. Wildcards and regex are supported.

instance.description: Filter logs by the instance they were emitted from.

label.description: The label to list values for.

level.description: Filter logs by their severity level. Wildcards and regex are supported.

method.description: Filter request logs by their requests method. Wildcards and regex are supported.

path.description: Filter request logs by their path. Wildcards and regex are supported.

resource.description: Filter by resource. A resource is the id of a server, cronjob, job, postgres, or redis.

startTime.description: Start time for log query (RFC3339 format). Defaults to 1 hour ago. The start time must be within the last 30 days.

statusCode.description: Filter request logs by their status code. Wildcards and regex are supported.

text.description: Filter by the text of the logs. Wildcards and regex are supported.
